Press & updates

The story is spreading beyond the build site.

From neighborhood reporting to regional media, this coverage tracks how tiny-home villages, community art, and grassroots organizing are reshaping what housing justice can look like in the Bay Area.

See featured coverage Volunteer with us
8
Stories and video features
3
Formats: print, audio, and film
1
Growing movement for housing justice
Tiny Village Spirit Bulletin Press File
Coverage documenting the build, the art, and the organizing behind each village.
In the press ★ Bay Area ★
Why this matters

Coverage helps neighbors picture a different housing response.

Each story helps more people see that tiny-home villages can be dignified, beautiful, resident-centered, and built through real community partnership.

Featured coverage

A closer look at the Richmond build
and the movement around it.

Audio report · KCBS

An empty lot transforms into tiny village for homeless youth in Richmond

A radio look at how the Richmond site is being transformed into dignified emergency housing for young people.

Listen to the report →
Video feature · YouTube

Fences: The First Step To A Tiny Homes Village In Richmond, CA

A video update from the Richmond build site showing how the project began taking shape on the ground.

Watch the full video →
